### Post by Dirty-Harry on 2005-07-09
I finally got my CDs in 2 days ago. I installed ubuntu and this problem has been extremly annoying. I have searched but no luck. i want 1024 x 768 but the resolution is stuck at 640 x 480 and there is no there other resolution in the drop-down menu.  here is my xorg:


### Post by jcohen on 2005-07-09
That's strange. Your /etc/X11/xorg.conf file looks good. Try reconfiguring xorg

Type "sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg" in a terminal and choose 1024x768 as the resolution you want to use. also make sure you set the monitor as supporting at least 1024x768. You can use defaults for other settings.

---

### Post by Dirty-Harry on 2005-07-09
Thanks for the help but could you yell me **how** to choose my desired resolution?  :-? I entered the code in the terminal but it never gave me the option

---

### Post by angkor on 2005-07-09
Use the search button on the forum, it's been covered. ;)

You need to enter your monitor's VerticalSync and HorizontalRefresh rates to get the desired resolution.
You can find both in the specs of your monitor.

---

### Post by philw on 2005-07-09
Things are not so simple with the Intel 810 driver (and Dell BIOS).

The following threads might be relevant to your chipset but I'm not sure. I'm using the Intel  82852/855GM chip with the same 810 driver but I needed the 855resolution fix before I could get access to the resolution needed.

As I understand it, Xorg or the 810 driver probes the BIOS for the available resolutions but the Dell BIOS does not report the available resolutions correctly. The 855resolution patch is a workaround.

[http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=24923](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=24923)
[http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=27029](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=27029)

Hope this helps.
PhilW

---
